triage.name: cand-D-choreography
triage.oneLine: Choreography as the syntactic spine of dregg2: G (annotated global type) + endpoint projection = the missing front-end over the A⊕B⊕C substrate, with blue/red projection-split, open-world mechanisms, and 2026-06-02 as-built receipts
triage.keyDesigns: Core thesis: choreography is a diagram in the turn-category; projection Choreo→∏ᵣEndpointᵣ is the functor; the runtime monitor of G↾p IS the vat-boundary membrane. Three unifications: (1) three per-turn judgements → one annotated G (conservation=linear payload typing, ordering=G's causal structure, I-confluence=blue/red BEC colour); (2) membrane = EPP monitor = cand-B verifier (blame = de-jure/de-facto split); (3) deferred coordination module → the front-end compiler. Blue/red routing: BlueEligible:=Confluence.IConfluent, blue→CellProgram admissibility (partition-tolerant, no commit), red→N-ary Hyperedge (generalization of bilateral JointTurn). Open-world dissolution via four mechanisms: compositional G (no single script), bottom-up compatibility synthesis (mpst-meet-communicating-automata), gradual ?-typed boundary, dynamic join/leave. Key honest tensions: (a) Byzantine equivocation gap — monitoring catches protocol-violation-as-observed, blocklace is needed for equivocation (cand-D bottoms out on cand-C); (c) Byzantine-EPP-by-monitoring is the central open theorem (not proved — named as obligation in Projection.lean:121); (d) projector is untrusted (soundness-by-verification — a bad projector produces turns the StepInv rejects, so TCB does not grow). As-built: Coordination.lean (GlobalType/project/Projectable/ProtocolCell:356/projection_sound), Projection.lean (blue_merge_safe PROVED, route, epp_correspondence as head-duality alias), Spec/Choreography.lean (red_projects_to_hyperedge/blue_needs_no_hyperedge/red_iff_coupled all proved + #assert_axioms-pinned; Hyperedge.lean for N-ary generalization). Verdict: adopt as intended front-end, build last, make G mandatory = kill switch.

triage.name: DREGG4-CROSS-POLLINATION

Built as of 2026-06-02: DesignatedVerifier.lean (DischargedFor/Transferable/DesignatedFor/TransferDial), EpistemicDial.lean (Dial=fullDisclosure≥selective≥acceptanceOnly as proved LinearOrder). Kachina bi-stated cell (§5.1): bi-stated cell = public-shared Obs + private-local facet related by ZK transition proof — simultaneously gives privacy a clean architecture, gives I-confluence a typed home (private=always tier-1, public=contended), matches Midnight interop. CALM/Bloom monotonicity type (§9.1): auto-derive finality tier from CellProgram transition-set monotonicity (Bloom/Hydro analysis over the ~13-effect ISA), closes OPEN-PROBLEMS #7 (nothing stops balance≥0 at tier-1). KeyKOS factory emits confinement-bound attestation (§3.2): Refusal generalized from point to region — a badge proving what the sandbox CANNOT do (dual of permission badge); CSpace.lean substrate now exists but in-circuit mutable(minted)⊆authorized badge is unbuilt. CapDAG-spec attested configuration badge (§1.1): attest a whole constellation IS the machine described (correct-by-construction vs correct-step-by-step). Lazy proof-migration + transparency theorem (§4.1): proof-migration and schema-migration are the SAME lazy-migrate-on-read+transparency shape. Attested causal-provenance of message sends (§2.1): resolution-DAG as a transferable badge. Explicit traps: consistent global checkpoint (vs causal cut), GC via cooperative back-edge reporting, search/matcher in TCB.

triage.name: FOUNDATIONS-limits-tensor-simplicial
triage.oneLine: Categorical-foundations excavation of dregg2 through one lens: the wide-pullback / equalizer limit, the tensor non-finality obstruction, and the simplicial/∞-cell structure — every claim tagged REAL / DECORATIVE / ASPIRATIONAL against exact Lean file:line receipts.
triage.keyDesigns: Wide pullback over TurnId as the n-ary atomic joint turn (Hyperedge apex = legs_agree cone collapse, O(N²)→1 agreement win). Tensor non-finality: the TRUE obstruction is that n-cell is a PROPER SUBOBJECT of the N-fold product (hyper_not_all_admissible, hyper_binding_is_proper over Unit) — NOT that νF₁⊗νF₂ is non-final (that literal slogan is false and retired). Cross-cell soundness ≠ per-cell ∧ per-cell is therefore irreducible: the binding is an external hypothesis, never derivable. Bisimulation-to-free-Spec form proved REFUTED (hyperedge_sound_bisim_ill_posed). Simplicial reading is DECORATIVE as kernel structure (no face-maps/∂/SimplicialObject anywhere) but REAL-as-analogy (agreement = fill-height = connectivity, grounded in Goubault–Kniazev–Ledent–Rajsbaum EPTCS paper). Interaction complex is NOT a Kan complex — faces don't freely extend, that impossibility IS the content. Two orthogonal infinities: temporal νF (greatest fixpoint, proved via Paco, REAL) vs arity ∞-cell (global atomic turn = colimit over all cells, ASPIRATIONAL as object, REAL-as-impossibility — fillable single-machine, UNFILLABLE across a partition). The binding obstruction is NOT a distributed fact (proved over Unit). Φ: positional-authority → epistemic-authority is now PROVED under NonDegenerate hypothesis (phi_functorial, 2026-06-02 update). Dial-cube: Disclosure×Transferability×Agreement as a higher-order-turn orthogonal axis (REAL as directed poset-product, UNBUILT). 25-row REAL/DECORATIVE/ASPIRATIONAL summary table.

triage.name: study-category
triage.oneLine: Stress-test of the dregg2 categorical model — identifies where category theory is load-bearing vs decorative, and drove the key reframe of cross-cell turns as pullbacks not tensored coalgebra morphisms.
triage.keyDesigns: The single most important finding: νF₁⊗νF₂ is NOT the final coalgebra of joint behaviour — finality does not transport through the product (cf. mathematical-theory-of-resources, Selinger no-Δ/◇). What does tensor cleanly is the GUARD (▷X×▷Y ≅ ▷(X×Y), productivity iso, not finality iso). Cross-cell soundness sits OUTSIDE the single-cell coalgebraic frame: the binding is a morphism into an equalizer in the BASE category, not a clause of any structure-map c:X→FX. Now materialized as IsJointTurn := IsPullback (Categorical.lean:584) with jointTurn_universal/:602 + jointTurn_mediator_unique/:613; N-ary IsWideJointTurn/:646. Conservation = monoid-hom + invariance (Reading A, coherent); 'strong monoidal functor' to a discrete target is DECORATIVE and vacuous on morphisms — retire the framing. Predicate⊣Witness Galois: the Heyting residual IS attenuation (a⊓b≤c ↔ a≤b⇨c) — threads coherently from Laws → Core admissibility → Authority attenuation. I-confluence join-semilattice as a tier-1 STATIC TYPE ERROR (eligibility = well-formedness the type system can check). Four load-bearing uses of category named explicitly: tensor non-finality, no-Δ/◇, Heyting=attenuation, I-confluence-type-error. sound_of_step_complete was FALSE (refuted via Spec.Carrier=Empty) and REMOVED; correct keystone is stepComplete_preserves.

triage.name: DREGG2-GAP-MAP
triage.oneLine: Prioritized gap analysis: the FFI executable turn is a narrow 5-effect scalar kernel; 11 fills enumerate what real execution needs with criticality, size, dependency order, and PREREQ-vs-ABOVE-CORE split.
triage.keyDesigns: Central structural finding: FullAction has exactly 5 variants (balance/delegate/revoke/mint/burn); escrow holding-store, note nullifier-set, CG-5 half-edge, vat membrane, per-asset vector all PROVED but STRANDED outside the dispatch. triage.name: SHEAF-OF-VERIFIERS
triage.oneLine: The full synthesis document: organizes per-party verifiers as a (pre)sheaf over the simplicial-epistemic site, maps all four sheaf components onto existing Lean theorems, draws the REAL/ESTABLISHED/ANALOGY/POETRY line, and gives the disciplined build order.
triage.keyDesigns: Core architectural idea: replace the one universal Verifier with a (pre)sheaf of verifiers — stalk = DVKernel per party (DischargedFor V, DesignatedVerifier.lean:113), restriction = a.newCommit=b.oldCommit intra-cell + Σδ=0 cross-cell, gluing = proofForest_sound (ProofForest.lean:177). Site = the simplicial-epistemic complex: vertices=local states, simplices=compatible tuples, indistinguishability=shared faces; proven via Hyperedge wide-pullback + legs_agree. Crucial constraint: hyper_not_all_admissible (Hyperedge.lean:505) forces the sheaf to be a fibration over bindings, never a free complex. Current single-universal-verifier = constant sheaf (the degenerate special case); publicMode_collapses_to_universal proves this. The one buildable first theorem: proofForest_sheaf_sound — replace uniform StepProofValid fibre with per-node DischargedFor Vᵢ, keep Linked unchanged (verifier-independent), add substantive overlap-compatibility hypothesis (same verdict on shared commitment surface, NOT Vᵢ=Vⱼ), re-prove fullProofForestInv. Non-vacuity teeth: dial_endpoints_distinct (DesignatedVerifier.lean:346) exhibits a disagreeing verifier whose section is genuinely rejected. Build order: (1) proofForest_sheaf_sound → (2) hand-built Čech 2-term complex C⁰/C¹/δ⁰ → (3) functorial ρ/Presheaf object. Honest labels: H⁰/H¹ as objects = POETRY until coboundary complex built; mathlib's Sheaf.H is the WRONG tool (derived-functor Ext cohomology on abelian sheaf, needs Grothendieck topology + HasExt — the right path is a hand-built finite 2-term complex). Bugs/upgrades/heterogeneity are one phenomenon: different restriction-map data in a non-constant sheaf.

triage.name: DREGG2-FOUNDATIONS

triage.name: DREGG4-HYPERSYSTEM
triage.oneLine: Rigorous design exploration of two distinct complexes — the Disclosure×Transferability×Agreement dial-cube (COMPLEX 1) and the interaction simplicial complex (COMPLEX 2) — their bridge via Agreement=fill-height, and the single-machine vs distributed dichotomy.
triage.keyDesigns: Two complexes kept separate: COMPLEX 1 is a finite directed poset-product (cube of lattices), COMPLEX 2 is a chromatic simplicial complex. Hyperedge (wide pullback over TurnId, Fin n generalization of JointTurn) is already built and proved axiom-clean — hyperedge_sound at Hyperedge.lean:374. Apex dissolves O(N²) pairwise bookkeeping but does NOT dissolve CG-2⊗CG-5 binding (hyper_binding_is_proper:164 proves proper subobject — irreducible residue). Interaction complex is a fibration over the bindings, not a Kan complex / free simplicial object. Agreement dial = simplex fill-height = common knowledge: tier-1=0-simplices, tier-2=k-set agreement, tier-3/4=top-simplex fill (common knowledge). Simplicial-epistemic paper tie: distributed knowledge D_B = shared higher-dimensional face; dregg hyperedge incidences = chromatic vertices; legs_agree = distributed knowledge of apex tid. Impossibility surface: deniable×high-agreement is empty (agreement fights deniability — REAL constraint). Single-machine = full hypersystem (pure fully-connected complex, every simplex fillable, #2 liveness impossibility collapses at n=1). Distributed = partition-bounded sub-complex (Agreement dial capped at maximal fill-height connectivity allows). First concrete build step: add Agreement coordinate to Turn.attest + Discharged[V] verifier-indexed + encode achievable sub-complex as order-ideal + deniable_high_agreement_empty refutation lemma.

R5: agents are FIND, kernel is VERIFY (LLM = untrusted soundness-only search plugin; zero authority by construction). R7: debugger = hot-patcher = one fork primitive (fork-on-input is time-travel; fork-on-program is hot-patch; both are cand-A §6 fork). Build-order spine: JointTurn trunk first → two dials as theory → runtime-theorem features (single-cell debugger/migration) → integration product (multi-agent sandbox) → cross-cell lift → surface (cand-D, shell).

triage.name: DREGG4-UNIFICATION
triage.oneLine: The foundational dregg4 thesis: the turn as a guarded comodel of a small effect theory with three faces as projections of c : X → F X, a 3-cube attestation modality, and a concrete unified Lean-ish type sketch collapsing the token zoo + storage + ~24 passthrough effects.
triage.keyDesigns: Central identification: EFFECTS = codomain action (AdmissibleTurn ⇒ X), CAVEATS = domain restriction (the predicate carving AdmissibleTurn out of AllTurn), ATTESTATION = Obs component — three components of c : X → F X, not three separate machines. Turn as dependent lens: get=attestation (Obs), put=effects (state update), domain-of-put=caveats. Turn as guarded comodel of effect theory T: cell is T-comodel (cohandles operations against state), turn = one step of cohandling, caveats = equations/guards, attestation = Moore output. One soundness theorem not three: comodel bisimilar to golden-oracle comodel with StepInv as contractivity — three faces are conjuncts of StepInv by construction, cannot drift apart. capExercise = lens composition (recursive inner-effect gating is compositional structure of lens, not special case). Transferability dial (genuinely new, zero exists): public/designated(V)/deniable(ring) as modal index on Obs; Obs becomes Obs[t:Transferability]; Discharged becomes Discharged[V] (verifier-indexed bisimulation). Third dial — Agreement/finality on same footing as disclosure+transferability: attestation is a point in Disclosure×Transferability×Agreement 3-cube (named new, not in any candidate). Concrete unified type sketch: inductive Core (~13 shapes), inductive Caveat + CaveatChain with tail:Hash (HMAC chain integrity), structure Attest with disclosure+transfer dials, structure Turn with effects/guard/attest. Collapses: token zoo → CaveatChain with KeyRef root (macaroon=HMAC/biscuit=Ed25519); ~24 passthrough effects → metaBind(tag,hash); storage → DSL-userspace over core. Accountable anonymity as potential fourth face (escrowed de-anonymization capability, capability-gated). User-extensible effects via theory-extension-with-comodel-homomorphism-proof (research-grade, last).

triage.name: CARRY-FORWARD-SYNTHESIS
triage.oneLine: Top-level synthesis of the three-faced turn model (effects/caveats/attestation) and the dregg2→dregg4 path, distilled from the four grounding docs

Repudiation dial as a new axis orthogonal to disclosure: transferability ∈ {public, designated, deniable} — Discharged must become verifier-indexed. dregg4 = one generator, three faces, two dials (transferability + disclosure), no bespoke 54-effect VM or ad-hoc token system. Phased path R→Caveat→Per-asset-balance→Repudiation-dial→Cross-cell-half-edge→DSL-demotion.

triage.name: GROUND-AUTH-ATTESTATION
triage.oneLine: Deep Rust-grounded audit of dregg's authorization and attestation layer: macaroon HMAC chains, 3P discharge protocol, credentials/selective-disclosure/anonymous-multishow, stealth auth, StarkDelegation, WitnessedReceipt, and the full repudiation/deniability analysis
triage.keyDesigns: Macaroon: Ti=HMAC(Ti-1,Ci), append-only append narrowing, constant-time tail-compare, typed dregg caveat vocabulary (App/Service/Feature/ValidityWindow/Budget etc.). 3P discharge: encrypted ticket/VID via ephemeral key r, XChaCha20-Poly1305, bind-to-parent (SHA256(root_tail)), 300s freshness — full Schnorr-style protocol. Credential layer: real STARK-backed selective disclosure (Poseidon2 revealed-facts commitment), predicate proofs (Gte/Lte/InRange), anonymous unlinkable multi-show with per-show blinding factor. Stealth: Monero/EIP-5564 X25519 DH + Ed25519 point-addition, P=H(r·V)·G+S, view tags. StarkDelegation: hides delegator/bearer behind root_issuer_commitment, only public scope bound to proof PI. Lean fidelity table: 5 major overlooks (no HMAC chain integrity, 3P discharge is a Bool flip, no selective disclosure in Credential.lean, multi-show unlinkability disconnected from credential path, Stealth+StarkDelegation dropped from 6-mode model). Lean LEADS on CapTP non-amplification (granted≤held proved, Rust missing it — real bug). Repudiation: dregg is hardwired to maximal transferability (no ring sig, no chameleon, no DVZK anywhere, grep-confirmed). Three deniability modes sketched: DVZK companion (OR-composition circuit), deniable MAC on CapTP handoff layer replacing Ed25519 on private path, ring-based repudiation extending BlindedSet machinery.

triage.name: EFFECT-ISA-DESIGN

triage.name: PHASE-PROOF-CARRYING
triage.oneLine: Full architecture for proof-carrying forests without recursive compression: ship the whole per-step proof DAG + linking witnesses, aggregate later as a pure performance swap.
triage.keyDesigns: ProofForest artifact shape: Vec<ForestNode{proof,public_inputs,WitnessAvailability}> + Vec<LinkEdge{Sequential|CrossCell}> + Vec<CrossFamily{members,shared_turn_id}>. verify_forest algorithm: (1) N independent verify_effect_vm calls; (2) intra-cell chain-link: new_commit(i)=old_commit(j), prev_receipt(j)=H(receipt_i), sovereign_seq(j)=seq(i)+1; (3) cross-cell CG-5: Σδ=0 + CG-2 turn-hash agreement; (4) DAG acyclicity + root anchoring. Soundness factors as (cryptographic per-proof) × (combinatorial linking) — the linking side is ALREADY PROVED in Lean (execForest_attests/crossForest_attests/forestAbsRun_forward). Silver Vision = carry full trace + re-run AIR; Golden = recursive proof node-local swap with unchanged verifier. Aggregation is a node-local artifact swap provably not changing the soundness statement. Privacy cost = DAG topology leaked; amount-privacy recoverable via Pedersen δ-commitments with Σ=0 in the exponent. # cand-D — Choreography as the syntactic spine: design recovery

## 1. The core design — the idea in its own terms

dregg2's synthesis converged on **"turn is the generator, and cell/cap/proof are three projections of it."** That is a claim about *semantics* — the smallest morphism the whole system is built from. cand-D fires at a **completely different altitude: syntax, the programming model — the thing a human writes and the thing a compiler reasons over.** Its one-line thesis:

> **A choreography is a diagram in the turn-category; endpoint projection is the functor from that diagram to per-cell behaviours; the runtime monitor of a projected local type *is* the vat-boundary membrane.**

So it is **not a rival to "turn is the generator."** It is the **missing front-end** whose back-end is the entire A⊕B⊕C substrate. The picture is two layers that coexist:

- **Bottom-up (dregg2):** you write a *turn* (one morphism); the executor composes them; the compiler checks `StepInv` per turn; coordination is *deferred*.
- **Top-down (cand-D):** you write a **global type `G`** (a multiparty protocol — who talks to whom, in what order, with what branching/recursion); the compiler reasons over `G`'s well-formedness and its **projection** to endpoints; coordination is **the front door.**

Categorically, the bridge between the altitudes is literal: `𝒯` = the turn-category (objects = cell-states, morphisms = turns); a choreography is a **diagram** in `𝒯`; endpoint projection `⟦·⟧ᵣ : Choreo → ∏ᵣ Endpointᵣ` is the **functor** sending `G` to each role's local behaviour; the runtime *executes the projection* by emitting turns/JointTurns. `G` **elaborates into** a composite of turns, and **projection is the elaborator** — exactly dregg2's "many syntaxes, one semantics" (Lean = semantics, metaprogramming = elaborator). A `JointTurn` — which dregg2 defines as the equalizer of N per-cell steps over a shared turn-id — is just **one interaction step of a projected `G`.**

### The headline structural move: 3 dynamic judgements → 1 typed artifact + 1 analysis

dregg2 carries **three side-conditions on every turn**. cand-D's central claim is that an **annotated global type `G`** absorbs all three, discharged *once* by the compiler instead of re-proven per turn:

| dregg2's per-turn judgement | becomes, in `G` |
|---|---|
| **Conservation (Law 1)** | **linear payload typing in `G`** — resources flow through the protocol linearly (Move-style); the linear-logic reading of session types. Checked once at type-check, lives *in the types*. |
| **Ordering (Law 2)** | **the causal/sequencing structure of `G` itself** — the protocol *is* the order. Intra-protocol canonicity is free; cross-protocol finality still needs the consensus tier. |
| **I-confluence (3rd judgement)** | **a static analysis on `G`** — projection-time **blue/red colouring**: interactions whose write-sets are BEC-I-confluent are *blue* (partition-progressing, no commit); the rest are *red* (atomic JointTurn). |

This is the cathedral move: confront the hardest deferred problem (coordination) by making it the **organizing principle** (the compiler), not a side-module.

### The runtime: how `G` becomes execution

```
   write G ──projection (the compiler)──► per-role local types ℓᵣ
                                                   │
                  ┌────────────────────────────────┴────────────────┐
                  ▼ blue interactions                   red interactions ▼
        CellProgram admissibility               JointTurn / N-ary Hyperedge
        (I-confluent, tier-1, NO commit,         (coupled, CG-2 ⊗ CG-5 equalizer,
         partition-tolerant)                      atomic, committed at tier-join)
                  │                                              │
                  └─────────── monitored boundary ──────────────┘
                     = the vat membrane (= cand-B verifier);
                       blame on non-conformance (de-jure / de-facto)
```

- **Blue** interaction → a `CellProgram` admissibility clause: runs cross-group, partition-tolerant, no commit. Eligible **iff** its write-set invariant is I-confluent.
- **Red** interaction → a **JointTurn / Hyperedge**: atomic, committed at the join of the written cells' tiers.
- **The boundary** between two endpoints → a **monitor** enforcing each side's projected local type — a proof-carrying turn whose conformance check *is* the witness.

## 2. The key moves / insights worth never-losing

These are the load-bearing ideas — the reasons this candidate matters:

1. **Membrane = endpoint projection = runtime monitor = cand-B verifier — one object at three names.** Projecting `G` to role `r` gives exactly what `r` may observe and must do across the boundary. A projected local type can be **enforced by a runtime monitor** that checks each message and **assigns blame on violation**. That monitor *is* dregg2's vat-boundary. And **blame = the de-jure/de-facto split**: "the protocol said you *may* do X; you didn't; here is the proof." The vat-boundary law and the EPP-correspondence theorem are *intended to be the same theorem at two altitudes* — `boundary_law` is the per-endpoint instance of `epp_correspondence`.

2. **The projector is UNTRUSTED — the TCB does not grow.** The deepest objection to a compiler-centric design is "you just moved all the complexity into a huge load-bearing projector." The answer is **soundness-by-verification** (cand-B): the projected output (CellPrograms + JointTurns + monitors) is *independently checkable*, so a **buggy projector produces a turn that fails the existing `StepInv` check.** The projector is outside the trusted base. This is what makes cand-D *purely additive and deferrable* — a bad `G`/projector can never compromise soundness, only fail loudly.

3. **The open-world dissolution — four composing mechanisms (this is why cand-D ≠ pyana-#1 reborn).** The fatal classical objection: choreographies assume a *closed world of known participants pre-agreed on one global script*, but the vision is "concurrency among strangers." The literature dissolves this:
   - **No single script — protocols compose** at typed interfaces (a *web* of small composable choreographies, not one cathedral-spanning `G`).
   - **Bottom-up compatibility replaces top-down agreement** — independently-specified endpoints can be *checked* for safe interaction without a pre-agreed `G` (MPST-meets-communicating-automata). Strangers don't agree on `G`; they check their locals are *compatible*.
   - **The typed/untyped boundary is gradual** — a dynamic `?` type lets typed and untyped endpoints interoperate, with blame when an untyped party deviates. This is *exactly* "choreography = the typed overlay; ocap messaging = the untyped substrate," formalized.
   - **Dynamic participation** — join/leave, optional roles, unbounded/indexed role populations; any *compatible* endpoint substitutes safely.

4. **THE design law that keeps it honest, and the one thing that kills it.** *Choreography is the typed, verified overlay you **opt into**; open-ended ocap messaging (cand-C turns/caps) is the substrate it compiles to and **falls back to**.* Two strangers interact via raw caps/turns with no shared `G`; when they agree, they pin a `G` and *gain* the static guarantees + a monitored boundary. **Make `G` mandatory and cand-D dies** (it regresses to a closed committee — punting the very openness the system exists for). This is a *social/engineering* risk, not a technical one: it is always tempting to "just require the protocol everywhere."

5. **cand-D is NOT self-sufficient — its Byzantine-safety bottoms out on cand-C.** This is the candidate's load-bearing honesty. Monitoring gives safety against *protocol-violation-as-locally-observed*, but a **Byzantine peer can equivocate** — show different messages to different observers — which *local* monitoring **cannot** catch. The fix is **not** in the choreography layer: it is the **blocklace/BEC substrate**, which makes equivocation harm "only a finite prefix." Clean split: *monitored projection handles "does this peer follow the protocol as I observe it"; the blocklace handles "does this peer show everyone the same thing."* Remove the blocklace and cand-D breaks. It is a **front-end**, and it owns that.

6. **The central OPEN theorem (named, not faked): Byzantine-EPP-by-monitoring.** Classical EPP assumes endpoints follow their projected types; a Byzantine endpoint deviates. The soundness theorem "monitored+blocklace-backed projection of `G` *refines* `G* over Byzantine parties" is **genuinely new** (monitor + blame + blocklace, not a typing theorem) and **not proven** — it is the candidate's frontier, the same one `STUDY-projection-split` already named.

# DESIGN RECOVERY — "FOUNDATIONS: limits, the tensor non-finality, the simplicial / ∞ structure"

`( ◕‿◕ )` Here's the idea, brought back vivid. This is one of the load-bearing lenses on dregg2's foundations — and it's the *good* kind of foundational doc, the kind that tells you exactly which of its beautiful words are paid for in proof and which are still IOUs.

## (1) THE CORE DESIGN — the idea in its own terms

**The single load-bearing categorical fact in dregg2 is a *negative limit fact.*** That's the whole spine. Let me unfold it.

A cross-cell atomic interaction — N cells all firing *one shared turn* atomically — is modeled as a **wide pullback over `TurnId`** (an N-fold fiber product). The Lean object is `Hyperedge` (`Hyperedge.lean:80`):
- `x : ι → T.Carrier` — the N participant pre-states (the legs' domains),
- `tid : TurnId` — **the apex** (literally Mina's `account_updates_hash`: one hash that every party's update commits to),
- `agree : ∀ i, turnId i (T.next (x i) t) = tid` — **the cone condition**, CG-2: every leg factors through the *one* apex,
- `balanced : Σ_{i∈univ} halfEdge i (x i) t = 0` — CG-5, the conservation aggregate: one finite monoid-sum over all incidences balances to zero.

So far this is just a construction. The **first move** is that the limit's universal property does *real work*: `Hyperedge.legs_agree` (`:111`) proves *any two legs agree* (`(H.agree i).trans (H.agree j).symm`) — the `O(N²)` pairwise-agreement data of a "family of binary edges" is **recovered for free from the single apex**. The cone collapses. One apex, free pairwise agreement. That's the limit *paying rent*.

But the heart — the reason the whole edifice exists — is the **second, negative move**. The naïve coalgebraic slogan would be: "`νF₁ ⊗ νF₂` is the final coalgebra of the joint behaviour, so cross-cell soundness = per-cell ∧ per-cell." **This is the load-bearing lie, and the design retires it in two steps:**

1. The *literal* slogan "the product of two finals is not final" was itself **mis-stated and is false** — the product of two final coalgebras *is* final for the product functor. The doc honestly burns its own earlier framing (and the retraction lives verbatim in the code, `JointTurn.lean:322-329`).
2. The *true* content is a **PROPER-SUBOBJECT fact**: the binding (CG-2 ⊗ CG-5) carves a *proper equalizer subobject* out of the product carrier `ι → T.Carrier`. The admissible joint configurations are **strictly fewer** than all product configurations. There exist product states the binding **excludes**.

This is the precise sense in which `⊗` fails to be a categorical product: *pairing is not surjective onto admissibility.* If `⊗` were the classifying product, every product state would reconstruct a valid joint state from its two factors. It doesn't. The correlation that CG-2 ⊗ CG-5 carries is **forgotten by the per-cell projections** — so it can never be a derived lemma. **The binding is an irreducible external hypothesis.** Cross-cell soundness ≠ per-cell ∧ per-cell, *forever*, and the category is what proves you can't cheat your way around it.

The payoff theorem `hyperedge_sound` (`:374`) then says: IF every incidence is per-cell step-complete AND the hyperedge binding `H` holds AND `Good` is preserved by every step, THEN `Good` holds along the *entire* run. And its proof is **one line** — it reduces to the single-cell `Boundary.stepComplete_preserves` on the product coalgebra, with product step-completeness discharged by `hyper_stepComplete` (all N legs at once, `∀ i`). The limit dissolved the bookkeeping; what it *cannot* dissolve is the binding-as-premise. That asymmetry is the entire thesis.

**Then two infinities, held strictly apart:**
- **Temporal νF** (greatest fixpoint, "forever"): a cell is live codata, a point of `νF` where `F X = Obs × (AdmissibleTurn ⇒ X)`. Soundness is a statement over unbounded time. This is **REAL** — proved coinductively, axiom-clean, via Lean-4.30 native `coinductive` + the vendored Paco (`gupaco`/`gpaco_clo`). This is the ∞ dregg2 *has*.
- **Arity ∞-cell** (colimit over ALL cells): the global atomic turn where the incidence set is *every cell in the system* — one apex `tid`, one global `Σ=0`. This is **Mina's one global ledger**. It is *not* a Lean object. But the reason it's missing is itself a theorem: it's **fillable on a single machine** (pure, fully-connected complex; a write-point always exists) and **UNFILLABLE across a partition** (atomic-cross-group ∧ partition-tolerant ∧ live is the classic distributed-commit impossibility). The ∞-cell as object is aspirational; the *biconditional* "fillable iff the complex is connected" is the rigorous content.

These two are **dual**: the limit (wide pullback) is the *spatial / cross-cell* atomic structure of ONE turn; νF is the *temporal* unfolding of ONE cell over all turns. And `hyperedge_sound` composes them — it runs the limit's apex tuple along νF's `Run`.

## (2) THE KEY MOVES / INSIGHTS WORTH NEVER LOSING

1. **The category earns its keep by *forbidding the tempting wrong factoring*, not by decorating proofs it already has.** This is the philosophical core. The value of the categorical lens here is a *prohibition*. `joint_sound_needs_binding` / `hyperedge_sound_needs_binding` prove there is **no** "both step-complete ⇒ joint-admissible" theorem. If you believed the slogan and conjoined two single-cell soundness proofs, you'd ship an **unsound** Boundary module — two cells locally step-complete while the shared turn doesn't exist (mismatched `tid`) or doesn't balance (`Σ≠0`).

2. **The proper-subobject obstruction is NOT a distributed fact.** `hyper_binding_is_proper` is proved over **`Unit`** — the most single-machine setting imaginable. This severs two things people always conflate: single-machine collapse removes the **liveness** obstruction (#2, the partition bound), but **never** the **binding** obstruction. On one machine the binding is *cheap* (synchronously suppliable) — never *absent*. You still must actually compute the shared `tid` and check `Σ=0`. The limit's proper-subobject content holds **at every dimension and every topology.**

3. **The bisimulation form is genuinely ILL-POSED, and the Lean proves the refutation rather than papering over it.** `hyperedge_sound_bisim_ill_posed` (`:433`): instantiate the spec coalgebra at `Spec () = ⟨Empty, …⟩`; then "bisimilar to a free spec" is *uninhabited* (no `y : Empty`) while every premise is satisfiable. The wide-pullback apex does NOT rescue it — the obstruction is the *free Spec*, not the binding. **The right shape is the SAFETY form (`hyperedge_sound`), not a bisimulation-to-free-Spec.** This is the discipline at its sharpest: a categorical shape (final coalgebra / bisimulation) that *fails*, proved-failed.

4. **Agreement = simplex fill-height = connectivity** — the one place the simplicial reading is more than decoration. A `Hyperedge` over `ι` is a global-state simplex; its incidences are agent-coloured vertices; the apex `tid` is the shared global fact; `legs_agree` *is* the statement that all N agents have distributed knowledge of `tid` (the simplex is *filled*). Agreement-tiers map to fill-height: tier-1 causal = 0-simplices, tier-2 ack-threshold = a k-face, tier-3/4 = the top facet (common knowledge). Grounded in a *cited* theorem (Goubault–Kniazev–Ledent–Rajsbaum, *Simplicial Models for the Epistemic Logic of Faulty Agents*, arXiv:2311.01351v3) and it makes a **falsifiable prediction**: a partition disconnects the complex, so the spanning simplex can't be filled — which *is* the partition bound.

5. **The interaction complex is NOT a Kan complex — and that impossibility IS the content.** A genuine simplicial object would need face maps `∂ᵢ` (restrict to `ι'⊆ι`), degeneracies `sᵢ`, and the simplicial identities as proved lemmas. The *first* honest theorem it would require is **negative**: a face of an admissible hyperedge is admissible *iff* its own CG-5 sub-sum is 0 — which it generally is NOT (a subset of a balanced set is unbalanced — that's `hyper_not_all_admissible` again). Faces **don't freely extend**, because each face is a proper subobject needing its *own* binding. A "full simplicial object with free fillers" would be **DECORATIVE & UNSOUND** — it would assert exactly the wrong factoring.

6. **`validity ≠ canonicity = "a simplex can be filled two ways."** `hyperedge_is_validity_not_canonicity` exhibits two distinct admissible hyperedges sharing a pre-state — two valid fillings of one boundary. That's the double-spend shape. Choosing one is *consensus*, delegated to `Finality`. Validity is a local decidable proof-property; canonicity is a vote. The category cleanly says *where* consensus must live.

7. **ember's single-machine principle, made rigorous.** The bounds at higher cells are *distributed* bounds. `n=1` collapses the liveness ones (single machine fills every simplex up to the ∞-cell synchronously) but leaves the binding ones standing. Distributed dregg is a **partition-bounded sub-complex** whose maximal fill-height equals current connectivity.

The structure is a layered argument, bottom to top:

**Floor — REAL physics (`OrbitalScreen`).** The sharp technical risk in any conjunction screen is *discretization error*: you sample the trajectory at instants and miss a between-samples closest approach. The fix is genuinely beautiful and genuinely sound. Model the relative motion of a satellite pair over one maneuver step as **affine**, `d(t) = d₀ + t·v` (the exact first-order structure of linearized Clohessy–Wiltshire/Hill relative dynamics). Then squared separation `‖d(t)‖²` is a **quadratic in t** — an upward parabola, `a = ‖v‖² ≥ 0`. A parabola's continuous minimum over `[0,T]` is at one *computable* point: the **vertex `-b/2a` clamped into `[0,T]`** — the time of closest approach (TCA). Check separation *there*, and you've certified the **entire continuous interval** with a single point-check. That's `screen_clear_imp_continuous_clear`: a clear verdict over-approximates the conjunction set, sound on the continuous orbit, not just at samples. The fallback for non-affine motion (`coarseClear`) uses a Lipschitz/speed-bound reverse-triangle inequality `sep(0) − v_max·T ≥ thr`, sound for *any* speed-bounded C¹ trajectory.

**Tie-break — graph rigidity (`WhoYields`).** Answers "is multi-agent even the right tool?" with a theorem. The conjunction graph's vertices are satellites; refine colors by neighbor-color multisets. If the refined coloring is **WL-discrete on conflict edges** (the scenario is asymmetric), then who-yields is **forced** — distinct roles, unique yielder by "lower role yields," *no central authority, no negotiation*. And the teeth: `symmetric_needs_negotiation` proves negotiation is load-bearing **exactly at the WL-indistinguishable cells** — a theorem about *where the deterministic referee runs out of forcing power*. **Spine — sheaf of verifiers (`EpistemicSheaf`).** The distributed-epistemic heart. Consensus on a clearance deal = a **global section** (H⁰ content): a screen-discharged clearance is **distributed knowledge of the honest operators**, world-independent so it survives every partial-knowledge edge, reached with **no central cop**. A Byzantine fork = a **witnessed non-gluing** (H¹ content): two operators each locally valid but disagreeing on the overlap fail to glue — the obstruction lives *entirely in the overlap disagreement*.

**The deep seed — `ConservationBridge`.** This is the part to never lose. dregg2 separately verifies an OS execution theory (`JointCell`: a committed bilateral maneuver has signed half-edges `halfA = -amt`, `halfB = +amt`, and `halves_sum_zero`, the Σδ=0 conservation law CG-5) and a graph-symmetry theory (`WhoYields`). # SHEAF-OF-VERIFIERS — design recovery (Opus, deep)

( ⌐■_■ ) The egg grows many eyes. Here is the idea, brought back vivid — and one thing the doc itself doesn't yet know: **its headline open theorem got built 20 minutes after the doc was last saved.** More on that under §C.

## A. THE CORE DESIGN — in its own terms

The whole move is a refusal: **a real metatheory of distributed systems must not assume a single global `Verifier`.** A global oracle is exactly the thing that doesn't exist in a decentralized world — and baking one in is what silently hardwires non-repudiation, homogeneity, and "everyone trusts the same checker" into the foundations.

The replacement structure is a **(pre)sheaf 𝒱 of verifiers** over a **site of parties**. Four data, each pinned to a dregg object:

1. **The SITE** = the *simplicial-epistemic complex* of who-shares-context. Vertices = local states (colored by owning party); simplices = compatible tuples of local states that can coexist in one global state; facets = possible worlds. The deep import (Goubault–Kniazev–Ledent–Rajsbaum, an *equivalence* not an analogy): **pure chromatic simplicial complexes ≃ proper S5ₙ epistemic frames**, with `w ∼ₐ w′` (party `a` can't distinguish two worlds) ⟺ **the two facets share the vertex colored `a`.** *Indistinguishability is shared faces.* The site literally **is** the who-knows-what structure. In dregg the site is the proof-forest's happened-before DAG (path-shaped today) and the N-ary `Hyperedge` (the simplicial joint-turn, a wide pullback over `TurnId`).

2. **The STALK** over a party = that party's **own VerifierKernel** — its verdict-space, the set of verdicts *its* verifier can issue. This is the one place the verdict genuinely depends on *who checks*: `DischargedFor V stmt proof := (verifyFor V stmt proof = true)`. The germ of the section at the stalk over `V`.

3. **The RESTRICTION MAPS** = how a verdict restricts to a shared overlap. The conceptual jewel: **agreement is "agree *after a restriction map*," not "hold identical private bits."** Two parties agree on shared edge `e` iff `ρ_{u⊴e}(x_u) = ρ_{v⊴e}(x_v)` — their *expressions on the shared topics* match. dregg has two restriction laws on its site: intra-cell **`a.newCommit = b.oldCommit`** (state continuity across the boundary commitment) and cross-cell **`Σδ = 0`** (the N-ary balance, valued in a commutative monoid).

4. **The GLUING / SHEAF CONDITION** = a *compatible* family of local sections (agreeing pairwise on overlaps) glues to a unique global section. Crucially, for finite graph-shaped sites this is **kernel-of-a-matrix finite** — no spectral sequence, no derived functors. `H⁰ = ker δ = Γ` = the global sections.

**The payoff that makes it more than vocabulary:** *software bugs, version upgrades, and genuine heterogeneity are not three concepts — they are one phenomenon.* They are different **sections** of the same verifier-sheaf, distinguished **only by their restriction-map data**. A bug = a section whose restriction disagrees on the overlap → fails to glue. An upgrade v1→v2 = a different section; backward-compat = restrictions still agree on the shared sub-context. Heterogeneity = the sheaf is honestly non-constant (different stalks per node). Byzantine lying = a restriction map that lies (the negative-scalar map: what C tells B need not match what C tells A). **One structure, four phenomena, zero bolt-ons.**

And the **cohomology is the invariant**: **H⁰ = the globally-agreed verdicts = consensus / common knowledge**; **H¹ = the obstructions = forks, bugs, version-skew, Byzantine disagreement.**

The single most beautiful structural claim: **today's single-universal-verifier is the *constant sheaf*** — the degenerate everyone-shares-everything-perfectly special case. "No global oracle" stops being a wish and becomes the *general* case of which dregg's current behavior is the constant restriction. That's the conceptual spine: *the global oracle is the degenerate case.*

## B. THE KEY MOVES WORTH NEVER LOSING

1. **The global oracle is the degenerate case, not the foundation.** Invert the usual framing: don't add heterogeneity on top of a universal verifier; recognize the universal verifier as the *constant section* `∀V. DischargedFor V` of a presheaf that is non-constant in general. This is the load-bearing reframe.

2. **Indistinguishability = shared faces.** The epistemic structure (who-can't-tell-worlds-apart) and the combinatorial structure (which simplices share a vertex) are *the same thing* (an equivalence, not a metaphor). The site doesn't model the epistemics — it *is* the epistemics.

3. **Agreement is "agree after a restriction map," never "hold identical bits."** This is what lets one structure absorb heterogeneity, version-skew, and Byzantine lying — they're all just *which restriction-map data* you carry. Equality-of-private-state was always the wrong notion of consensus.

4. **The fibration-over-bindings constraint.** `hyper_binding_is_proper` proves the hyperedge is a **proper subobject** — not every product tuple is a simplex (a face of a balanced hyperedge is generally unbalanced). So the only *sound* sheaf is a **fibration over the bindings, never a free complex**: covers are the bindings, and gluing across a partition can be genuinely *unfillable*. This is the dregg shadow of "the protocol complex is a proper subcomplex of the full join." It's what keeps the structure honest rather than vacuously total.

5. **The gluing must BITE.** A sheaf condition is only content if non-gluing is *witnessed*: `badNode` has each node individually valid (`StepProofValid := True`) yet they don't glue (`1 ≠ 99` at the overlap). This is the exact structural twin of Abramsky–Brandenburger contextuality: *a locally-compatible family with no global section.*

6. **The discipline line (the "kernel-exposes-gaps" rule).** Sheaf vocabulary must **generalize an existing term-proved theorem, never paper over a missing one.** Ship the gluing as a gluing (REAL); label cohomology as the next theorem (OPEN). Calling today's finite gluing "cohomology" would let vocabulary substitute for the absent coboundary — the doc refuses this, and that refusal is the most important methodological move in the file. The 4-tag ledger (REAL / ESTABLISHED / ANALOGY / POETRY) is the mechanism.

7. **The right cohomology tool is hand-built, not mathlib's.** mathlib's `Sheaf.H F n` is **derived-functor Ext-group cohomology** of an *abelian* sheaf on a Grothendieck-topologized site, needing `HasSheafify` + `HasExt` (enough injectives) — the heavy topos machine, and the *wrong invariant* for a finite party-complex. The honest path is a **hand-built finite 2-term complex** `C⁰ →^{δ⁰} C¹` on `Algebra/Homology`, where `H⁰ = ker δ⁰`, `H¹ = coker δ⁰`. Knowing *not* to reach for the obvious mathlib import is itself a hard-won insight.

8. **H¹ = fork is a *sound detector*, not an iff.** The lit itself proves the cohomological obstruction is **sufficient but not necessary**: a nonzero class certifies a fork, but a zero class doesn't certify agreement (the clean iff is "global section exists ⟺ non-contextual"; "⟺ H¹=0" holds only in good cases — possibilistic/ℤ₂/All-vs-Nothing). Never assert "fork = nonzero H¹" without the good-case hypothesis. The defensible statement: *consensus = H⁰ non-trivial and contains the desired section; a fork = that section doesn't exist, certified by a nonzero relative-H¹ class (sound, not complete).*

## (1) THE CORE DESIGN — in its own terms

**There is exactly one primitive in the kernel, and every "lens" is a projection of it.** Not six theories — one object seen six ways.

The object is a **guarded Moore coalgebra carrying a measure, an order, and an authority graph, whose soundness is a bisimulation to a golden oracle, conditional on a contractivity premise:**

```
  c : X ⟶ Obs × (AdmissibleTurn ⇒ X)
          └┬┘   └──────────┬─────────┘
      ATTESTATION    guarded DOMAIN (caveats) + CODOMAIN action (effects)
```

decorated with three side-structures the kernel quantifies over: a **measure** `count : · → M` (conservation, a monoid-hom), an **order** on attestations (`Tier`, a `LinearOrder`) and on caveats (Heyting residual `⇨`), an **authority preorder** (`confers`, a thin category), and a **resource algebra** (`Fpu` on an Iris camera) into which conservation and authority both collapse. A *cell* is a point of this coalgebra; a *turn* is the edge `next x t`; the perennial "is the primitive the cell or the morphism?" tension **dissolves** because the morphism IS the structure map, not a second object.

The **six lenses are six honest readings** of that one `F`:
- **coalgebra** — drop everything but `step : X → F X`; soundness = bisimulation (relational gfp + native `coinductive ObsBisim`).
- **effect-comodel-lens** — read `F` as a guarded comodel of an effect theory; three faces = put/guard/get.
- **limits-tensor-simplicial** — the n-ary atomic cross-cell turn = a wide pullback over `TurnId`; joint admissibility = a *proper equalizer subobject*.
- **modal-dials** — `Obs` carries Disclosure × Transferability × Agreement.
- **authority-cdt-camera** — the capability-derivation-tree is a thin category; the vat-boundary Φ is a named-lossy functor caps→keys; the camera unifies conservation+authority.
- **verify-find-logic** — TCB = the verifier; every search is an untrusted plugin emitting a checkable witness; seam = `verify ⊣ find`; proof-forest = a gluing.

**The discipline that makes it more than cosplay:** every structural claim is tagged **REAL** (a universal property/law actually proved with a non-vacuity witness, `file:line` = receipt), **DECORATIVE** (suggestive notation buying no theorem, says what it would have to prove), or **ASPIRATIONAL** (`sorry`/unbuilt). The single strongest evidence the discipline bites: **two slogans that would have papered over missing theorems were caught FALSE in the Lean and corrected**, not faked (see move #3 below).

**The ∞-cell answer — the question the whole exercise was for** — sharpened to one sentence: *"∞" is not one thing.* It splits along two orthogonal axes plus a third clean reading:

# DREGG4-OS-ENDGAME — design recovery

`docs/rebuild/DREGG4-OS-ENDGAME.md` (534 lines, read in full). A read-only "galaxy-brain" exploration of the OS-scale features that sit *above* the dregg2 kernel. Here is the idea, made vivid, then ground-checked against the Lean as it stands today.

---

## 1. The core design — in its own terms

**One sentence:** *dregg2 proved the local theorem; dregg4 is what you get when you take the single-cell theorems coinductive-and-networked, and the OS feature falls out of adding exactly one networked dial or one re-seed.*

The whole document hangs on a frame it inherits from the corpus — **the three-faced turn**. Every turn (and every feature) decomposes onto three faces:

- **EFFECTS** — the living-cell state transition (the A-projection / coalgebra step).
- **CAVEATS / AUTH** — authorization-narrowing (the capability CDT, attenuation-only).
- **ATTESTATION** — the badge = `permitted ∧ committed`, the *output* — and this face carries **two dials**:
  - **disclosure** ∈ {acceptance-only, selective, full} — *what* is revealed;
  - **transferability** ∈ {public, designated, deniable} — *to whom* the proof is convincing.

The thesis is that the *transferability* dial does not exist in the running system (pinned at `public` = non-repudiable), and **that single absence is the seam through which half the OS enters**.

The other load-bearing structural fact: **cross-cell binding is irreducible.** A multi-cell turn is a `JointTurn` — a span/pullback over a shared turn-id, not a tensored coalgebra — and "compose the per-cell theorem" *provably fails* there. The doc names this `νF₁⊗νF₂ is not final` and makes it the trunk: **everything hard bottoms out on the JointTurn binding.**

The discipline that keeps it honest (carried from `REORIENT §6`): for each feature, separate a *genuinely-new primitive* from a *rephrasing of an existing theorem*. The corpus's sharpest prior result is that **checkpoint / restore / replay / time-travel are theorems, not features** — they fall out of (anamorphism re-seed + retained log + a rollback-handler turn). So the dregg4 question is never "add the feature"; it is *"what is the smallest genuinely-new primitive or dial that turns an existing theorem into the OS-scale product, and where does it sit on the three faces?"*

The nine features it walks (migration, distributed debugger, cap markets, choreography front-end, multi-agent-on-untrusted-code, hot-patch, revocation/recovery, ocap shell) are each decomposed this way: ~80% "derives from an existing theorem," ~20% "one genuinely-new piece," and that 20% almost always turns out to be *the same two things* — the transferability dial, or the JointTurn binding.

---

## 2. The key moves / insights worth never-losing

The seven "galaxy-brain rethinks" (§9), which are the actual payload — the things the corpus *implies* but never names:

- **R1 — A tradeable capability is a note (the cap-nullifier).** The single most unifying move. *Exclusive transfer of a capability* — "I give you this cap and provably no longer hold it" — **is nullifier-spend** (the `NoteSpend` shape): transferring a cap *spends* the holder's copy into a nullifier set and mints the recipient's. This one primitive (a reuse of the existing note machinery, commitment-insert / nullifier-spend) **collapses three features into one**: capability markets, lost-device recovery, and exclusive hand-off. The surprise: the corpus *warns against* merging notes (privacy) and caps (authority) as conserved domains — but the resolution is that they are the same *mechanism* over different *records*; the warning was against merging the *conservation laws*, not the *shape*.

- **R2 — The transferability dial is the missing axis that unlocks half the OS.** *One* piece of new theory — a **verifier-indexed `Discharged`** (designated-verifier ZK) — is load-bearing for **four** features: migration receipts (convince B, not the world), debug views (I showed you my cell's state; you can't prove to anyone else what it held), accountable de-anonymization (reveal identity only to a named authority), and private name introduction. Disclosure controls *what* a proof reveals; transferability controls *to whom* it's convincing — independent axes (a proof can be fully ZK yet still non-repudiable).

- **R3 — Everything hard bottoms out on the JointTurn binding.** Migration-atomicity, network-fork, market-settlement, cross-cell upgrade, recovery-binding *all* reduce to the CG-2⊗CG-5 cross-cell binding, which is irreducible to per-cell soundness. The consequence that re-prioritizes the whole roadmap: **the JointTurn is the trunk of the OS, not a consensus branch.** Investing in it is investing in all of them at once.

- **R4 — Distributed consistent snapshot = the JointTurn equalizer.** Chandy-Lamport's "consistent cut" ≡ the CG-5 "conservation-balanced ∧ downward-closed cut." The debugger's distributed snapshot and the JointTurn's atomicity are *the same predicate*. The doc flags this identification as unstated anywhere — a dregg4-native idea.

- **R5 — Agents are FIND, the kernel is VERIFY.** An LLM agent is *definitionally* the "untrusted soundness-only search plugin" of the verify/find seam: the agent *searches* (writes code, finds a delegation path, proposes a protocol); the kernel *verifies*. The agent has **zero authority by construction** — it can only ever *fail to produce* a valid badge, never forge accept. This recasts "AI safety for coding agents" as a verify/find-seam *property*, not a sandboxing hack.

- **R6 — There is no global clock, so "now" is a cap.** With tier-1 causal-only finality, there is no global time; freshness/expiry/recency are all *relative to what a vat has learned*. So a "current epoch" assertion is itself a witnessed fact — **a beacon is a capability**, freshness is authority-gated.

- **R7 — The debugger and the hot-patcher are one primitive (fork).** Forking on the *input stream* = time-travel debugging; forking on the *program* = hot-patch; forking on the *schema* = migration. One operation — "fork the unfold, diverge one coordinate, merge under attenuation" — subsumes debugging, hot-patching, A/B-testing of cell rules, and speculative agent branches. The merge rule (*merge iff every edge stays a monotone attenuation*) **is** the security boundary: an agent literally cannot merge work that amplified its authority.

Two structural disciplines worth keeping alongside the rethinks:

- **The dials are first-class choices, not global settings** — proven by the fact that markets want `transferability=public` (settlement must be third-party verifiable) while debugging/migration want `designated` (the *opposite*). Same axis, opposite poles, per feature.
- **The crypto/Lean rail never merges** — the DVZK is a *circuit obligation*; the verifier-indexed `Discharged` is the *Lean law*. Search engines (market clearing, choreography synthesis) are *untrusted FIND plugins*. The TCB never grows. # Design Recovery: The Three-Faced Turn (CARRY-FORWARD-SYNTHESIS)

`(づ｡◕‿‿◕｡)づ` Found it. And here's the twist that changes everything about how to read it: **this doc is not a lost vision — it's a recently-executed battle plan, and the battle is largely won.** Dated 2026-06-01 (yesterday), it synthesized four grounding investigations into a ranked carry-forward checklist. When I grep the current Lean, almost every item it ranks as "THIN / OVERLOOKED / a fiction" now exists as a built, kernel-clean module that *cites this very doc by section number*. So the recovery has two layers: the idea (still vivid and correct), and the surprising news that the idea already grew legs.

---

## 1. THE CORE DESIGN — the turn is a generator with three co-equal faces

The doc's reframe, in its own terms: dregg's `EffectVmAir` lens trained everyone to see **a turn as "a list of effects."** That is one projection of a richer object. A turn is **one generator with three co-equal faces** — REORIENT's A/B/C projections made concrete:

| Face | What it is | Mechanism (Rust ground truth) | The badge it produces |
|---|---|---|---|
| **EFFECTS** | the state-transition (REORIENT A: living-cell step) | the effect VM + per-asset side-tables | the committed delta |
| **CAVEATS / AUTH** | authorization-*narrowing* (REORIENT B law + C authority-CDT) | macaroon HMAC chains, 3P discharge, selective disclosure, anon multi-show, stealth, StarkDelegation | "permitted" |
| **ATTESTATION** | the output badge (permitted ∧ committed) | WitnessedReceipt / publicly-verifiable STARK | "convincing-to-whom" |

The **headline finding**: the EFFECTS face had been grown deeply while CAVEAT and ATTESTATION stayed thin — *and on those two faces the Rust kernel is substantially richer than the Lean model.* So "the right basis" was never a better effect set. It was **all three faces modeled faithfully.**

**Two structural insights make this more than a slogan:**

**(A) The bidirectional carry-forward principle.** Fidelity is not one-directional. Two opposite failure modes:
- **Rust → Lean** (carry rich feature semantics *up*): the Lean is a *fiction* exactly where the Rust is rich. A caveat was a bare `Ctx → Bool`; the real macaroon is an HMAC chain `Tᵢ = HMAC(Tᵢ₋₁, encode(Cᵢ))`. 3P discharge was a `Bool` flip; the real thing is encrypted-ticket + VID + bind-to-parent + freshness.
- **Lean → Rust** (carry proven laws *down*): the Lean *leads* where it proved a law the Rust omits — CapTP `granted ≤ held` non-amplification is **proved in Lean, missing from Rust** `verify_captp_delivered`. That's a *real Rust bug the Lean caught.*

The discipline that ties it together: **"carry forward the Rust semantics" = the Rust's rich features + the Lean's proven core laws, reconciled into ONE faithful model — because the Lean will replace the Rust kernel.** Never model a Lean fiction; never launder a buggy oracle.

**(B) The repudiation dial — a genuinely new axis.** The sharpest original insight. dregg is **hardwired to maximal transferability ⇒ non-repudiable**: the running `presentation.rs::verify(&self)` takes *only the proof + public inputs* — no `verifier_secret` argument anywhere in the crate. The Lean mirrors this exactly: `Discharged p w := Verify p w = true` is a **single universal predicate, not indexed by who is checking.** So the model **cannot even express** "convincing only to verifier V." dregg HAS anonymity (hide *who*) but LACKS deniability and designated-verifier (hide *to whom the proof is convincing*) entirely — a real privacy hole for an anonymous-collaboration OS. The fix is **a new orthogonal axis, not a patch**: transferability ∈ {public, designated, deniable}, sitting beside the disclosure dial. **`Discharged` must become verifier-indexed.**

**The dregg4 telos:** one generator, three faces, **two dials** (transferability + disclosure), where *every* higher capability — storage, advanced credentials, deniable interaction, cross-chain — is composed from a small orthogonal effect core + the caveat algebra + the attestation modes. **No bespoke 54-effect VM. No ad-hoc token system bolted beside it.**

## 2. THE KEY MOVES WORTH NEVER LOSING

1. **The three-faced reframe itself.** "A turn is not a list of effects; effects are one of three co-equal faces." This is the load-bearing idea — it reorganizes the entire roadmap.
2. **Bidirectional fidelity.** Rust→Lean for features, Lean→Rust for laws. Neither side is the master.
3. **Transferability ⊥ disclosure.** *What* a proof reveals vs *to whom it is convincing* are independent axes. A proof can be fully zero-knowledge yet still non-repudiable. The model couldn't see this because `Discharged` had no verifier index — the collapse *hid the axis.*
4. **Per-asset conservation is the biggest soundness gap.** One scalar `bal` lets a cell silently swap asset 0 for asset 1 while the aggregate stays put. Conservation must be a *vector* indexed by `AssetId` — *every* asset preserved by *every* transfer.
5. **Storage = userspace, not kernel.** `CapInbox`/`PubSubTopic`/`BlindedQueue` are DSL programs over *existing* effects, not bespoke kernel features. **Small orthogonal effect core + verified DSL-composed userspace = the right shape (seL4-style).**
6. **The §8 portal discipline (the honesty rail).** Never fake crypto as proved. HMAC unforgeability, AEAD soundness, STARK extractability are `Prop`-carriers discharged by the Rust+circuits — you prove the *reduction* ("forge ⇒ break HMAC"), never the primitive. "An honest `-- OPEN:` beats a vacuous theorem."
7. **Shadows are the enemy.** The recurring villain is the "shadow": a Lean model that throws away the substance that makes the real thing work (the escrow shadow FID-ESCROW was the first one killed; caveat/discharge/disclosure were next). Every reconcile-step must "never reintroduce a shadow."

# PHASE-PROOF-CARRYING — Design Recovery

`/Users/ember/dev/breadstuffs/docs/rebuild/PHASE-PROOF-CARRYING.md` (2026-05-31, Opus read-only design pass). Here is the idea, vivid.

## 1. The core design — in its own terms

**The move: refuse to compress.** A turn — or a cross-cell turn-family, or a whole nested call-forest — is a **DAG of steps**. The reflexive instinct in a ZK system is to crush that DAG into one succinct recursive proof (IVC / STARK-in-STARK / folding). The doc's thesis is a refusal: dregg has *never* had a sound, useful version of that compression (it proves this in §2, file:line), so **don't make soundness depend on it.** Instead:

> Ship the **whole forest** of per-step proofs — each standalone, each independently verifiable — **plus the linking witness data** an aggregate proof would otherwise have swallowed into its public inputs.

This is **Proof-Carrying Data minus the accumulation step.** PCD's whole apparatus is the recursive verifier-in-circuit that lets node *i* attest "I checked node *i−1*'s proof." Strip that out. Keep the data it was carrying — the boundary commitments — *on the wire, in the clear*, and have a plain verifier check them combinatorially.

**The verifier does two passes:**
1. **Per-proof (cryptographic).** Verify *every* proof against its own public inputs (N independent `verify_effect_vm` calls — the "§8 seam," the one irreducibly cryptographic assumption).
2. **Linking discipline (combinatorial, no crypto).** Walk the happened-before edges and check that each proof's `NEW_COMMIT` equals the next proof's `OLD_COMMIT` (state continuity), the receipt-hash pointer chains (`prev_receipt(j) = H(receipt_i)`), the sovereign sequence ticks (`seq(j) = seq(i)+1`, no replay/fork), and that cross-cell edges *balance* (CG-5: `Σδ = 0` over the family, with CG-2 turn-hash agreement). Plus DAG acyclicity and root-anchoring to the last-attested commitment.

**The soundness of the composite factors cleanly:**

```
composite_sound  ⟸  (∀ node. per_proof_sound)   — (P) cryptographic, the §8 seam, ASSUMED
                  ∧  (∀ seq-edge. new = old ∧ receipt-link ∧ seq+1)  — (L_seq) combinatorial
                  ∧  (∀ family. Σδ = 0 ∧ CG-2 agree)                 — (L_×)  combinatorial
```

The right-hand conjuncts — the *linking* — are **pure combinatorics over felt vectors**, and the doc's load-bearing discovery is that **they are already proved in Lean** (the executable-model versions). So the architecture is not new mathematics: it is the **deployment shape** of theorems dregg2 already has, realized over cryptographic artifacts instead of `KernelState`.

**The killer property — aggregation becomes a strict performance swap.** Because the verifier reads *only* the PI projection (`OLD_COMMIT`/`NEW_COMMIT`/`δ`/…) and calls a backend `verify`, you can later replace a *sub-forest* `S` with a single node carrying a recursive proof `π_S` over `S` plus `S`'s **frontier** PIs (root old-commits, leaf new-commits, net δ). The forest verifier is **unchanged** — it just verifies one node's proof and checks the *frontier* links exactly as before. The soundness statement is *closed under this substitution*. So recursion, the day it actually works EffectVm-shaped, **drops in with zero changes to the verifier or the soundness proof.** It buys O(1) bandwidth and graph-topology privacy — and **nothing in the soundness column.**

## 2. The key moves / insights worth never losing

- **"The commitments that make linking possible are already in the proof's public inputs."** This is the hinge of the whole design (§1.1). The EffectVm AIR already exposes `OLD_COMMIT`, `NEW_COMMIT`, `EFFECTS_HASH`, `PREVIOUS_RECEIPT_HASH`, `SOVEREIGN_WITNESS_SEQUENCE`, and the `NET_DELTA` half-edge in its PIs. A proof already *says* "I took `OLD_COMMIT` to `NEW_COMMIT`, emitting these effects, at this receipt position." The linking surface is not something to add — it is **already protruding from every proof.** dregg is *de-facto* doing proof-carrying-minus-accumulation for single cells already; the only missing thing is to *name and check the edges*.

- **Soundness factors as (cryptographic) × (combinatorial), and dregg2 owns the combinatorial factor outright.** This is the load-bearing finding (§3.1). The abstract claim "a forest of per-step transitions that each attest `StepInv` and chain `new=old` / balance `Σδ=0` is sound as a composite" is *literally a Lean theorem already*, in both directions (intra-cell chain-link **derived**, cross-cell Σ=0 **hypothesized**), and **closed under iteration**. The new work is "a half-day Lean module," not research.

- **The intra-cell vs cross-cell asymmetry is real and principled.** Intra-cell, a single commitment *continues* (`new = old`) — so conservation is **derived** (every node is a balance turn on one cell). Cross-cell, there is **no global ledger**, so no single commitment continues; the link is the **CG-5 N-ary `Σδ = 0` binding**, which is *genuinely load-bearing and never derivable* — the doc insists on this and the Lean proves it (a δ-family summing to 3≠0 is excluded; `crossForest_needs_binding`). This is the deep structural truth: **distributed conservation is a binding you must carry, not a fact you can prove.**

- **The honest demolition of the existing recursion code (§2).** Three modules, three verdicts: `plonky3_recursion.rs` (`AggregationAir`) is a Poseidon2 *hash chain over the inner proofs' public inputs* — its own docstring admits "the verifier still needs access to the inner proofs for full soundness," so as an *aggregation* it saves nothing (it's the proof-forest with a digest bolted on). The `recursion`-feature FRI-in-circuit path (`plonky3_recursion_impl.rs`) is real but (a) wraps the *digest* AIR not the EffectVm AIR, (b) uses **toy** FRI params (`query_proof_of_work_bits: 0`, 2 queries), (c) the smoke test literally notes "the hash-chain computation is NOT enforced." `ivc.rs` says outright it's "implemented as a HASH CHAIN." **Verdict: none is a sound, useful *compression* that lets you drop the per-step proofs. Treat all of it as not-on-the-critical-path.** This is what *frees* dregg to ship on the post-quantum STARK now.

- **The §8 boundary is entered as a named hypothesis, never an `axiom`/`sorry`.** The per-node "this proof verifies and the AIR is sound ⇒ a real committed step exists" is the **declared cryptographic seam**, entered "exactly as the `CryptoKernel`/`World` portals enter their assumptions." This is the dregg2 house discipline: the crypto obligation is *visible as a hypothesis*, discharged in Rust by the FFI golden-oracle cascade, never laundered into the Lean proof.

- **The privacy accounting is honest about what the forest leaks (§6.1).** Shipping the tree **reveals the interaction graph**: how many steps, per-step commitments, effects-hashes, δ-magnitudes, edge structure. This is a *real cost, not a wash*. Mitigations recover *amount*-privacy without aggregation: commitments are already hiding (Poseidon2, ~124-bit), and δ can ride as **Pedersen commitments with the Σ=0 check done in the exponent** (the hidden-asset equality argument already exists, #84). But the **DAG shape** (fan-out, depth) stays visible — and hiding *that* is the **one honest reason aggregation is *desirable* later, not a correctness prerequisite.** This cleanly separates "what you need" from "what's nice."

- **Validity ⊥ canonicity (§7).** The forest is *purely the validity layer*. A proof attests "this is **a** valid history" — it *provably cannot* say "this is **the** canonical next history" (two equivocating valid turns each carry a perfect proof; the proof system is symmetric in them). Canonicity is the **separate** Cordial-Miners/blocklace machinery (τ-order debit, quorum at 2f+1). This is *why* the forest fits consensus cleanly: O(n) per-block validity is local and parallel, the linking discipline *is* the per-block invariant a miner re-checks on receipt — no global recursive proof to maintain across the DAG.

# PHASE-UC-TRANSPORT — design recovery

## The shape of the problem (why this phase had to exist)

dregg2's metatheory is **order-/realizability-theoretic**: a claim is "known" iff a *decidable* `Verify` oracle accepts a witness. That frame is gorgeous for everything that's a fixpoint or an order law — but it has a hard wall. `Metatheory/EpistemicConsensus.lean §6` (lines 335–391, read directly) names the wall precisely: the full **Canetti UC composition theorem**

```
(∀ Z, view_Z(π) ≈ view_Z(F)) → (∀ Z, view_Z(ρ^π) ≈ view_Z(ρ^F))
```

is stated as a **sharp `-- OPEN:`**. It cannot be a Lean theorem in this frame because `view_Z` is a *probability ensemble*, `≈` is *computational indistinguishability* (not a Lean order-law), and you'd need a simulator `S` plus a hybrid argument over contexts. The §6 OPEN explicitly routes that residue to the very same `Prop` carriers `Crypto/Primitives.lean` isolates: `CryptoPrimitives.binding` (DLog) and `CryptoPrimitives.unlinkable` (hiding/anonymity), lines 58 & 64 — *never proved in Lean, never `sorry`*.

So dregg2 had an honest hole with two dishonest ways to "close" it: write `axiom binding` (assert crypto on nothing), or write `sorry`. **The whole idea of this phase is a third way.**

## The core design — discharge-by-transport, not discharge-by-axiom

The move is: **don't prove UC in Lean — prove the load-bearing fragment of it in a tool that actually models probabilistic games, and thread that external proof into Lean as a *carrier whose inhabitation is the bridge act*.**

Three pieces, all of which I confirmed exist on disk:

**(1) The Isabelle side — the real game-based proof.** `~/dev/breadstuffs/uc-crypthol/Dregg2_FCom.thy` (206 lines, read in full). It identifies dregg2's `commit value blinding` with the AFP `Sigma_Commit_Crypto.Pedersen` commitment `commit ck m = g^d ⊗ ck^m`, *inside the `pedersen` locale* (prime-order cyclic group). Then it re-exports the already-machine-checked AFP Pedersen security:
- `dregg2_F_com_correct` ← `abstract_correct`
- `dregg2_F_com_hiding` / `dregg2_perfect_hiding` ← `abstract_perfect_hiding` (perfect, *unconditional*, advantage = 0)
- `dregg2_F_com_binding` / `dregg2_binding_reduces_to_dlog` ← `pedersen_bind` (binding advantage **= the DLog advantage of an explicit reduction** `dis_log_𝒜`)
- and the asymptotic locale `pedersen_asymp`: `dregg2_binding_under_dlog` (DLog negligible ⟹ binding negligible) + the realization bundles.

The genuinely clever structural insight buried here: the homomorphism is **transport fidelity made into a proof obligation**. `dregg2_commit_hom` (thy lines 72–89) *re-proves* dregg2's one PROVED-grade algebraic law (`commit_hom`, additive homomorphism) from the group structure on the Isabelle side. That's the load-bearing pin connecting "the dregg2 object" to "the Pedersen object" — the one law both sides must agree on, proved in *both* logics.

**(2) The Lean side — `FComDischarge` as a Type-valued structure (the key architectural move).** `Dregg2/Crypto/UCBridge.lean` lines 89–112. This is the piece the Sonnet triage correctly flagged as never-lose. It is **not an `axiom` and not a `sorry`** — it's a `structure ... : Type` bundling, as fields:
- the three carried `Prop`s (`correct`, `perfectHiding`, `bindingReducesToDLog`),
- their *proofs* (`correct_holds`, `hiding_holds`, `binding_holds`),
- and crucially the **entailments** `entails_binding : bindingReducesToDLog → P.binding` and `entails_unlinkable : perfectHiding → P.unlinkable`.

Then `binding_unlinkable_discharged_by_crypthol : FComDischarge P → P.binding ∧ P.unlinkable` (lines 134–136) is a genuine Lean theorem, kernel-clean, guarded by three `#assert_axioms` tripwires (lines 170–172).

The elegance: **the cross-system trust is reified as data you must construct, not a global axiom that silently poisons the kernel.** To get `P.binding` for a real primitive set, *someone has to hand you an `FComDischarge P`* — and constructing one is exactly the act of vouching (under the caveat) that the CryptHOL transport applies. The trust is local, named, and inspectable at every call site rather than ambient. That is the whole philosophical payload of the phase: **a carrier whose truth rests on a proof in another system, made syntactically honest.**

**(3) The honest trust ledger (§5 / the UCBridge docstring lines 29–46).** Accepting the bridge *widens* dregg2's trust base, stated explicitly, to: Lean kernel **+** Isabelle/HOL LCF kernel **+** AFP CryptHOL & Sigma_Commit_Crypto **+** the **human-checked** fidelity that dregg2's `commit` (with sole law `commit_hom` over an `AddCommGroup`) really *is* `g^d·ck^m`. That last one — "no verified Lean↔Isabelle translation" — is named as **the honest residual gap**. The framing line worth keeping verbatim: this is *strictly stronger* than a bare `axiom`/`sorry` (discharged by a real proof in a real UC tool) and *strictly weaker* than a single-kernel Lean proof (trust spans two kernels + transport fidelity).

## Insights worth never losing

1. **The "carrier discharged by another system" pattern.** This is reusable far beyond commitments. Any genuinely-cryptographic `Prop` carrier (collision-resistance, IND-CPA of the intent-seal AEAD, the anonymity advantage) can be discharged the same way: a Type-valued discharge struct with entailment fields, inhabited by transporting a CryptHOL/EasyCrypt proof. The bridge theorem stays kernel-clean.

2. **The homomorphism is the transport anchor.** Don't transport "the commitment" abstractly — transport *the one shared algebraic law* and re-prove it on both sides. `commit_hom` is the hinge; everything else (hiding, binding) hangs off the AFP library once the object is pinned.

3. **Perfect vs computational asymmetry, surfaced correctly.** Hiding is *unconditional* (advantage = 0); binding is *conditional* (= DLog advantage). The carriers `unlinkable` and `binding` therefore have different epistemic status, and the thy keeps them distinct — including the type-level reason they can't share an adversary.

4. **The real bug as a fidelity signal.** Reaching green exposed that the three *bundle* theorems had reused one adversary `𝒜` for both the hiding conjunct and the binding conjunct — but in `Commitment_Schemes`, `hid_adv` (a state-passing pair) and `bind_adversary` (a single opening-producing function) are **distinct types** ("Clash of types `_ × _` and `_ ⇒ _`"). Fixed by quantifying a separate binding adversary `ℬ`. This is a *real* soundness-adjacent lesson: a "commitment is hiding AND binding" bundle that lets one adversary witness both is type-confused — the games have different interfaces, and conflating them would have been a subtle laundering of the security statement. I verified the fix is live: the thy's bundle theorems (lines 141–145, 186–201) carry both `\<A>` and `\<B>`.
